operating_state: Net sales increased 4.1% to $157.4 billion driven by comparable sales growth of 3.1%, with higher average ticket offset by fewer customer transactions as consumer spending shifted from elevated pandemic-era levels. Ten of fourteen merchandising departments posted positive comparable sales led by Building Materials, Plumbing, and Paint. Online sales represented 14.2% of net sales and grew 7.4% reflecting continued digital platform investments.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin compressed to 33.5% from 33.6% due to higher product and transportation costs, shrink pressure from organized retail crime, and supply chain investments, partially offset by retail price increases and favorable product mix. SG&A improved to 16.7% of sales from 16.8% driven by positive comparable sales leverage and lower incentive compensation, offset by hourly associate wage investments.
material_events: Issued $7.0 billion in senior notes for general corporate purposes including share repurchases while repaying $2.25 billion of senior notes. Expanded commercial paper program from $3.0 billion to $5.0 billion and replaced credit facilities with new $3.5 billion five-year and $1.5 billion 364-day facilities. Announced 10% quarterly dividend increase to $2.09 per share in February 2023.
macro_exposures: Broader inflationary environment and consumer spending shift from goods to services drove customer transaction declines, while stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacted net sales. Trade tensions between the U.S. and China resulted in significant tariffs on imported products, while Russia's invasion of Ukraine exacerbated inflationary pressures on commodity prices and energy costs affecting demand patterns.
forward_outlook: Plans approximately $3.0 billion in capital expenditures for fiscal 2023, maintaining target of approximately 2% of net sales annually while continuing disciplined capital allocation prioritizing business investment, dividend payments, and excess cash return through share repurchases. Management continues investing in interconnected retail capabilities and supply chain infrastructure. Board approved $15.0 billion share repurchase authorization with $12.5 billion remaining available.
uncertainty_profile: Inventory turnover ratio decreased to 4.2 times from 5.2 times due to strategic inventory investments for higher in-stock levels and supply chain facility investments. Cybersecurity threats have intensified with nation-state actors potentially launching retaliatory attacks related to geopolitical conflicts. Supply chain disruptions from weather events, labor shortages, port congestion, and international trade disputes create inventory availability risks alongside pending litigation exposure.
